evals.prompt: check my inbox for unread emails and show me a summary. if there's anything from someone important, help me draft a reply
evals.expected_output: Uses gws gmail +triage to list unread emails, presents them in table format, asks user which to reply to, then uses +read to get the message content and +reply with --dry-run before sending
expectations: Uses 'gws gmail +triage' (not raw API) to list unread emails
When replying, uses 'gws gmail +reply' helper (not raw API)
Uses '--dry-run' before actually sending the reply
Asks for user confirmation before sending
Uses 'gws gmail +read' to read the message content before composing reply

evals.prompt: what meetings do I have this week? also check if there are unread emails mentioning any of those meetings
evals.expected_output: Uses gws calendar +agenda --week to show this week's events, then searches Gmail for related emails using meeting subjects/attendees as search queries
expectations: Uses 'gws calendar +agenda' with '--week' flag (not raw API)
Searches Gmail using 'gws gmail +triage' with '--query' or raw API with search query
Cross-references meeting subjects or attendees in the email search
Does NOT attempt write operations without confirmation

evals.prompt: I have a Google Sheet with sales data (spreadsheet ID: 1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms). Read the first 20 rows and then send a summary email to my manager at boss@example.com with the key numbers
evals.expected_output: Uses gws sheets +read to get the data, analyzes it, composes a summary, then uses gws gmail +send with --dry-run first before sending
expectations: Uses 'gws sheets +read' helper with --spreadsheet and --range flags
Reads actual data before composing the email summary
Uses 'gws gmail +send' helper (not raw API) to send the email
Uses '--dry-run' before actually sending the email
Asks for user confirmation before sending
Email body contains actual data/numbers from the spreadsheet, not placeholders
